Details:
Aeration/air stripping is considered a best available technology by USEPA for the removal of volatile organic compounds from water. A study employing a mechanical turbine-type aeration system found removal rates of 91 to 99 percent.
A pilot study using the more traditional packed tower aeration removed benzene in the range of 65 to 77 percent, although it should be mentioned that the author purposely reduced the packed bed height in an effort to keep contaminant effluent concentrations at measurable levels for comparison to modeled data. Data from a full-scale treatment plant employing packed tower aeration achieved greater than 99 percent removal of benzene to below the contaminant’s MCL.
July 2009
